items.title: Iran War Pushes Chinese Travelers Closer to Home
items.excerpt: Security fears have done what even visa hassles and high prices never could, convincing the world's biggest tourism market to just stay closer to home.

items.title: Steve Case’s Onefinestay Is Shedding Rentals to Chase Luxury Travelers: Exclusive
items.excerpt: Onefinestay, a luxury vacation rental brand, will cede management of 200-plus homes to Veeve. The move is part of a tactical shift for a brand that had an urban and less exclusive tilt under its previous owner.

items.title: NYC Finds 27% of Approved Short-Term Rental Listings Are Now Illegal
items.excerpt: Turns out you can probably find some entire-home short-term rental listings in New York City, after all, because hosts take lawfully registered listings and convert them into illegal ones. Airbnb isn't required to take any action to limit the practice — and apparently isn&ap

items.title: United Airlines Cuts 2026 Profit Outlook Due to Rising Fuel Costs
items.excerpt: United is the first U.S. carrier to lower its 2026 outlook as a result of surging fuel prices. Alaska Airlines, which reported first-quarter earnings on Monday, suspended guidance for the year.

items.title: Trump Tells Transportation Department to ‘Take a Look’ at Spirit as Bailout Talk Grows
items.excerpt: It is unclear if the federal government would actually give Spirit a bailout. Trump signaled earlier that he thinks “maybe” the federal government should help.

items.title: World Cup Travelers Squeezed By More Than Just Ticket Prices
items.excerpt: It’s more than just ticket prices that soccer fans will be paying up for at this year’s World Cup. Hotel taxes and transportation costs are just some of the ways organizers and hosts are squeezing money out of spectators.

items.title: Aviation’s Decarbonization Bet Is Looking Shakier Than Ever
items.excerpt: The Middle East fuel crisis has accidentally made the case for SAF better than any policy ever did — and revealed exactly why the industry can't act on it.
